focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Elizabeth jests about finding Bess a husband while Walsingham urges practical matches; Bess desires an honest, attractive man, as Elizabeth humorously considers foreign rulers for marriage.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Elizabeth's favored maid of honour
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Bess states her personal criteria for a husband when Elizabeth discusses finding her a match.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To marry a man she personally desires, based on her stated criteria.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Bess has expressed her marital preferences to Elizabeth, who responds humorously and maintains affection.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Elizabeth will arrange a marriage that aligns with Bess's personal desires.
evidence_quotes: She leans over to brush a stray lock of hair from Bess's face.
BESS I'll want the marriage if I want the man.

relation_updates: Elizabeth shows intimate favor by brushing Bess's hair
Bess asserts personal agency in marriage discussion with Queen
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A maid who asserts personal choice in marriage, as shown in her dialogue with Elizabeth.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Raleigh dramatically lays his cloak for Queen Elizabeth, who steps over it skeptically; amused Bess watches as Elizabeth enters the chapel, leaving Raleigh to reflect on his failed gesture.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Observer of courtship rituals
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Bess watches Raleigh lay his cloak for Elizabeth and shares a quick smile with him after Elizabeth walks over it.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Bess has observed Raleigh's gesture and exchanged a smile with him before continuing with the royal party.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Bess's smile indicates romantic interest in Raleigh
evidence_quotes: Bess Throckmorton watches, suppressing a smile.
Bess throws Raleigh a quick smile as she follows the royal party.

relation_updates: Bess and Raleigh share a moment of mutual understanding

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Raleigh presents gold taken from a Spanish ship to Elizabeth, who acknowledges it as piracy; he defends it as protecting England, earns her approval, and is welcomed home despite her teasing about his attire.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Confidante and messenger for the Queen
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Bess is warned by Elizabeth for tardiness, then taken into confidence to view suitor portraits, offers a candid opinion on Archduke Charles, and is entrusted with carrying a summons.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Bess has been entrusted with a personal errand after offering her opinion on the suitor portraits.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Elizabeth's warning that future tardiness won't be forgiven
evidence_quotes: ELIZABETH Late again, Bess.
Elizabeth takes Bess's arm and turns with her to the portraits.
BESS He's rather sweet.
Bess understands. She bobs and hurries to the door.

relation_updates: Receives warning from Elizabeth about tardiness
Taken into personal confidence about marriage prospects
Entrusted with carrying the Queen's summons
status_updates: Functions as both lady-in-waiting and personal messenger

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Elizabeth confides in Walsingham, calling the Archduke a sweet boy and expressing concern she does not want him harmed by Walsingham's schemes, adding tension beneath courtly decorum.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Lady-in-waiting acting as intermediary for Elizabeth
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Elizabeth commands Bess to talk to Raleigh, and Bess engages him in conversation, advising him to speak plainly to win the Queen's favor.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Bess has conversed with Raleigh and provided advice on gaining Elizabeth's favor.
evidence_quotes: ELIZABETH (low) He interests me. Talk to him.
Bess has now approached Raleigh. BESS The pirate is not too bored by the vanities of the court, I hope.

relation_updates: Bess is commanded by Elizabeth to engage with Raleigh
Bess and Raleigh have a meaningful conversation deepening their connection

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Bess refuses to help Throckmorton return to court, fearing betrayal and danger, despite his plea that they will convert to the new faith and their lives depend on her.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Bess is confronted by her cousin George Throckmorton, who pleads for her help to return to court.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Bess refuses to assist George Throckmorton, citing loyalty to the Queen and fear of personal danger.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Bess has declined the request, straining her relationship with George and leaving the Throckmortons' appeal unanswered.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether the Throckmortons will be caught and killed, as their fate is tied to Bess's refusal.
evidence_quotes: BESS: I cannot betray the Queen.
BESS: Don't put me in danger like this.
BESS: I'm sorry.
THROCKMORTON: If they catch us, they will kill us. Our lives are in your hands.

relation_updates: Refuses to assist George Throckmorton, straining familial bond
Reaffirms loyalty to Queen Elizabeth
status_updates: Takes a stand against aiding known Papists
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Prioritizes loyalty to the Queen and personal safety over familial obligations when faced with risky requests.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 52、INT. QUEEN'S PRIVATE QUARTERS, WHITEHALL PALACE - DAY
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Walsingham reports that the traitor Throckmorton has confessed, prompting fear in Bess and anger in Elizabeth, who steps away from Raleigh to hear the news privately.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Lady-in-waiting to Queen Elizabeth, with a relative (Throckmorton) who confessed to treason.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Bess displays a flash of fear upon hearing Walsingham report that Throckmorton has confessed to treason.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Bess is visibly fearful, and the Queen moves away to discuss the matter privately with Walsingham.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The Queen's reaction to Bess's familial connection to the traitor is pending.
evidence_quotes: A flash of fear in Bess's eyes.

relation_updates: Fearful awareness of potential guilt by association with the Queen
status_updates: Position now precarious due to family connection to confessed traitor

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Bess, consoled by Raleigh after confessing her guilt over betraying her cousin, finds solace in their long-repressed passion, leading to an intimate embrace.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Bess confesses guilt and seeks comfort from Raleigh
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Bess tells Raleigh she informed on her cousin, leading to his execution, and they share a passionate kiss
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Bess and Raleigh have shared an intimate embrace, releasing pent-up passion
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Bess's guilt over her cousin's death persists; their new intimacy is secret
evidence_quotes: Bess is on her knees before the simple altar, her head bowed in prayer.
A man was hanged today. A Papist. I knew him well. He was my cousin. He died because I gave information.
Suddenly they're kissing eagerly, greedily, their long pent- up passion released at last.

relation_updates: Relationship with Raleigh becomes intimate
Trust deepened through shared vulnerability
status_updates: Emotional burden of guilt partially relieved through confession

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Bess wakes to find Walsingham watching her; he accuses her of betrayal, referencing her affair with Raleigh, and demands she inform him to stay safe.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Lady-in-waiting confronted by Walsingham about her affair with Raleigh
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Walsingham wakes Bess, accuses her of betrayal due to her affair with Raleigh, and orders her to keep him informed
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Bess is terrified and knows she must inform Walsingham to stay safe
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How to comply with Walsingham's demand while protecting Raleigh
evidence_quotes: On the far side of the room, sitting watching her in silence, is Walsingham.
WALSINGHAM But you have, my dear. And you do. We both know that.
Bess knows he means her affair with Raleigh.
WALSINGHAM Keep me informed, and all will be well. I don't like surprises.

relation_updates: Walsingham gains coercive leverage over Bess
Bess's affair with Raleigh is exposed to the spymaster
status_updates: Under surveillance and threat from Walsingham
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Terrified and defensive lady-in-waiting

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Bess reveals her pregnancy to Raleigh, urging him to forget her and fulfill his duty, as she leaves to join the Queen's summons, leaving Raleigh resolute with a new determination.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Pregnant ward of the Queen facing legal constraints on marriage and childbearing
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Bess discloses her pregnancy to Raleigh, states her plan to ask the Queen for permission to leave court and live at her mother's house, and asks him to forget her and do his duty.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To obtain permission from the Queen to leave court and raise her child quietly at her mother's house.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Bess leaves to answer the Queen's summons after the disclosure.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Bess can secure permission to leave without the Queen discovering her pregnancy.
evidence_quotes: BESS: I'm – Her hand touches her waist. RALEIGH: You're pregnant?
BESS: To my mother's house.
BESS: I ask that you forget me. Go to your ship. Do your duty. Forget me.

relation_updates: Discloses pregnancy to Raleigh
Explicitly asks Raleigh to forget her
Acknowledges legal constraints as Queen's ward
status_updates: Pregnant with Raleigh's child
Planning to leave court permanently
Facing severe consequences if Queen discovers truth

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Raleigh and Bess secretly marry with a priest and maid of honor Margaret present; Raleigh places the ring on each finger while reciting vows, ending on the ring finger.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Secret bride
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Bess marries Walter Raleigh in a private chapel ceremony, with a priest officiating and Margaret as the sole witness.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Bess is secretly married to Raleigh, with the union concealed from the court.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The secret marriage risks discovery and potential royal displeasure.
evidence_quotes: Raleigh and Bess are being married in secret by a PRIEST.
The only witness is Bess's fellow maid of honour Margaret.

relation_updates: Married to Sir Walter Raleigh
status_updates: Married woman
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Bride in a clandestine marriage ceremony

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Elizabeth, confronted by Raleigh's quiet courage, regains composure and orders Raleigh's arrest for seducing and marrying Bess without consent, asserting royal authority.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Maid of honor confronted by Queen Elizabeth
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Queen Elizabeth violently confronts Bess about her secret marriage and pregnancy, beats her, and orders Raleigh's arrest.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Beaten by the Queen and publicly disgraced; her husband Raleigh is arrested.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The consequences of Raleigh's arrest and the Queen's ongoing anger.
evidence_quotes: BESS Yes, my lady. It is - my husband's child.

relation_updates: Relationship with Queen Elizabeth becomes hostile
Publicly acknowledged as Raleigh's wife
status_updates: Pregnancy revealed
Falls from royal favor
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Maintains dignity under physical and verbal assault from the Queen.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Queen Elizabeth blesses Raleigh's newborn son, reflecting on her role as a mother to her people and valuing their love above all else.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Mother curtseying to Queen Elizabeth and receiving a touch of forgiveness
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Queen Elizabeth touches Bess lightly on the cheek after Bess curtseys, silently forgiving her
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Bess is forgiven by the Queen, and her child is blessed
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The stability of the forgiveness and the future of Raleigh's ambitions
evidence_quotes: Bess rises at once and comes through to make her curtsey to the Queen.
The Queen puts out her hand and touches Bess lightly on one cheek.

relation_updates: Relationship with Queen Elizabeth is partially reconciled
status_updates: Mother of a blessed child
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Mother who sought and received forgiveness from the Queen

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Bess Throckmorton begins as Elizabeth's favored maid of honour who asserts personal agency in marriage discussions. She develops a secret romance with Raleigh while maintaining her position as trusted confidante, but faces crisis when her cousin's treason and Walsingham's surveillance threaten her standing. After secretly marrying Raleigh and becoming pregnant, her disgrace is violent when discovered, but Elizabeth ultimately forgives her after the birth of her son.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Elizabeth defends Mary Stuart as a cousin and guest despite ministers' warnings that she endangers England as a Catholic figurehead and potential martyr.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Queen Elizabeth defending Mary Stuart against ministers' calls for action.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Elizabeth asserts Mary Stuart's status as cousin and guest, and challenges advisors on the justification for harming her.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Elizabeth maintains her protective stance towards Mary Stuart, resisting pressure from advisors.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How to handle Mary Stuart without making her a martyr.
evidence_quotes: Mary Stuart is my cousin. She is our guest. And she is under our control.
You'd have me make a martyr of her. What is her crime?

relation_updates: Reinforces kinship with Mary Stuart
Shows tension with advisors Howard and Hatton

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Elizabeth refuses to punish Catholics for their beliefs, emphasizing justice for actions alone and maintaining her people's love through restraint and fairness.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Queen refusing to punish Catholics for beliefs, only for actions, in response to advisors' fears.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Elizabeth asserts she will not punish people for beliefs, only for deeds, and emphasizes earning the people's love through fairness.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To enforce laws based on actions alone and to earn the love of her people.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Elizabeth has stated her policy of tolerance based on deeds to her advisors, countering their fear-based arguments.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The tension between advisors' fear of Catholic threats and Elizabeth's insistence on tolerance remains unresolved.
evidence_quotes: If any of my people break the law, they will be punished. Until that day, I wish them to be let them alone.
I will not punish my people for their beliefs. Only for their deeds.
My constant endeavour is to earn that love.

relation_updates: Counters advisors' fear-based approach
status_updates: Affirms role as a fair and loving ruler
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A ruler who prioritizes justice based on actions and seeks to earn love through restraint.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Elizabeth removes her makeup by candlelight, observing her aging face; she exchanges a rare smile with Bess, then grows somber, gazing at her vulnerable reflection.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Elizabeth privately confronts her aging reflection during makeup removal with Bess.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Elizabeth observes lines on her face, exchanges a smile with Bess, and then becomes somber while gazing at her vulnerable reflection.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Vulnerable and reflective, gazing at her naked face in the mirror.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Her aging and the passage of time.
evidence_quotes: ELIZABETH Lines round my mouth. Where did they come from?
With that she smiles, and sees Bess's answering reflected smile.
Her smile fades. She gazes at her now naked face, vulnerable in the mirror.

relation_updates: Shows intimate trust with Bess during makeup removal and shared smile
status_updates: Confronts her own mortality and the physical signs of aging

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Philip sacrifices forests to save England's soul, viewing deforestation as divine light; Reston sees the burning woods as purification and demands to be sent home.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Subject of Philip's marriage proposal and accusations
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Philip states that Elizabeth rejected his marriage proposal and defended virginity, which he uses to justify his resentment and the felling of forests.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Elizabeth's rejection is confirmed, provoking Philip's hostile actions as depicted in the scene.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Ongoing conflict with Philip over marriage and religion.
evidence_quotes: PHILIP (V.O.) `Elizabeth! You are leading the souls of your people to Hell! Turn back! Marry me, and save England!'
PHILIP But she did not listen. She made me a speech about the virtues of virginity.

relation_updates: Philip resents Elizabeth for rejecting him

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Elizabeth assesses defense plans amid foreign threats; suitors from Sweden and Russia present marital and geopolitical challenges; court factions vie for influence as national security and stability hang in the balance.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Queen receiving briefings on threats and evaluating defense plans amid suitor presentations in court.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Elizabeth is briefed by Walsingham on famine in Ireland and French instability, examines dock defense models with Howard, and reacts dismissively to suitor portraits from Sweden and Russia.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Elizabeth has inquired about Thames defense and is presented with multiple foreign suitor portraits.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Ongoing famine in Ireland, potential rebellion, and unresolved marriage proposals from foreign powers.
evidence_quotes: Walsingham reports that thousands are dying every day of famine in Ireland and warns that another rebellion is a distinct possibility.
Elizabeth turns to Lord Howard, asking if he has the plans for the new docks.
Elizabeth studies the model carefully, her concern shifting to national defense.
Elizabeth expresses exasperation, asking if he is still pursuing her.

relation_updates: Elizabeth shows personal concern for Bess
Elizabeth receives warnings about Ireland and France from Walsingham
status_updates: Elizabeth studies dock model and inquires about defense against enemy ships
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Elizabeth shows pragmatic concern for national security and personal interest in Bess's whereabouts.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Queen Elizabeth departs as Bess and Raleigh share a moment of mutual attraction, with Bess teasing gratitude, before the Queen calls Raleigh.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Queen formally declining a marriage proposal and summoning Raleigh
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Elizabeth declines Archduke Charles's marriage offer, stating she lives for her people, and after the audience, calls for Raleigh.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Austrian marriage proposal declined; Raleigh summoned by the Queen
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Elizabeth's future marriage prospects and her interest in Raleigh
evidence_quotes: The Queen lives for her people. You will therefore forgive me, sir, if after much thought and prayer I decline your offer of marriage.
Elizabeth rises and takes the Archduke's arm as they start to leave the chamber.
The Austrian entourage is just leaving as the Queen turns. ELIZABETH Mr. Raleigh.

relation_updates: Closes door on Austrian marriage alliance
Signals continued interest in Raleigh
status_updates: Remains unmarried queen

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Elizabeth confides in Raleigh about her need for admiration and longing for adventure, urging him not to leave as tensions rise.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Queen appointing Raleigh as Captain of her guard and confiding her vulnerabilities
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Elizabeth knights Raleigh as Captain of her personal guard and urges him not to leave, revealing her need for admiration and fear of abandonment.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Raleigh is knighted and appointed to Elizabeth's guard, but her plea for him to stay remains unaddressed.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Raleigh's desire to sail to the New World versus Elizabeth's request for him to stay.
evidence_quotes: Elizabeth is pacing, disturbed, Raleigh's letter open in her hand.
I have decided to appoint you Captain of my personal guard.
Rise, Sir Walter Raleigh.
Please don't leave me now.

relation_updates: Appoints Raleigh as Captain of her personal guard
Knights Raleigh
Confides personal vulnerabilities and dependence on Raleigh
status_updates: Raleigh knighted
Elizabeth reveals emotional vulnerability and need for admiration
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Elizabeth shows both authority and personal insecurity in her appeal to Raleigh.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Babington attempts to assassinate Elizabeth in the chapel, but the pistol misfires; overwhelmed, he collapses as Elizabeth remains unharmed and composed.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Queen surviving an assassination attempt in the chapel
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Elizabeth turns to confront Babington's gun, the pistol misfires leaving her unharmed, and she remains frozen after the attack.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Unharmed and frozen in place, staring into space.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Babington is incapacitated, but the broader conspiracy threat may persist.
evidence_quotes: Elizabeth turns and rises to confront the assassin's gun.
She's unhurt.

relation_updates: Adversarial relationship with Babington confirmed as he attempts to kill her
status_updates: Survives an assassination attempt unharmed
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Composed and unharmed after the attempt.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Elizabeth refuses to sanction Mary Stuart's execution, asserting royal authority over the law, while Walsingham insists on legal necessity before withdrawing.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Sovereign asserting royal authority over the law
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Elizabeth refuses to sanction Mary Stuart's execution, declaring that the law does not apply to princes and dismissing Walsingham's counsel.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Elizabeth has asserted her sovereign will against the execution, and Walsingham has withdrawn after being dismissed.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The demand for Mary Stuart's execution persists, as Walsingham insisted on legal necessity before leaving.
evidence_quotes: Must die? Mary Stuart must die? Where is it written? Who dares to give orders to the Queen?
Don't preach at me, old man. Look at you! You can hardly stand. Go home to your wife. Go home to your bed.
The law is for common men, not for princes.

relation_updates: Elizabeth dismisses Walsingham with contempt

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Elizabeth realizes she was manipulated into executing Mary, an anointed queen, and faces the moral and divine consequences, preparing for the impending conflict.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Queen confronting Walsingham about manipulated evidence leading to Mary's execution
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Elizabeth learns from Walsingham that Mary's execution was based on manipulated evidence, recognizing it as a sacrilegious act that justifies Philip's holy war, and begins to prepare for the conflict.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Elizabeth is left alone, burdened by the realization and steeling herself for the coming battle.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The moral reckoning for executing Mary and the justification for Philip's holy war.
evidence_quotes: Elizabeth slowly grasps the horrifying truth: she ordered Mary’s execution based on manipulated evidence.
Overcome with dread and moral reckoning, she acknowledges that divine retribution may be imminent.
Elizabeth barely acknowledges his departure, lost in the weight of her realization.

relation_updates: Elizabeth's trust in Walsingham is shaken
Elizabeth now sees Philip's holy war as justified
status_updates: Elizabeth gains moral awareness of her sacrilegious act
Elizabeth prepares for the impending battle for her throne and soul
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Composed and intense, facing the truth with dread.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Elizabeth's forces at Tilbury number 3-4,000; Walsingham warns that if the Spanish fleet reaches Calais, their combined armies will be unstoppable, so it must be prevented.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Queen Elizabeth leads a strategy session with advisors to address the Spanish Armada threat.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Elizabeth takes control in the map room, assesses enemy forces, and sets the objective to prevent the Spanish fleet from reaching Calais.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Prevent the Spanish fleet from reaching Calais to avoid a combined invasion.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Elizabeth has established the primary military objective to block the Spanish fleet at Calais.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The Spanish Armada's advance and the defense against potential naval breakthrough.
evidence_quotes: Elizabeth is imperious, back in control.
Therefore, the Spanish fleet must not reach Calais.
This Spanish Armada is at sea carrying an army of ten thousand men.

relation_updates: Elizabeth works with her advisors (Walsingham, Howard, Hatton) in a coordinated strategy session
status_updates: Elizabeth is back in control and imperious
Elizabeth sets a clear military objective
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Imperious and back in control.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 132、EXT. HILL ABOVE THE ENGLISH CAMP - DAY
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Queen Elizabeth, in silver armor on a white horse, unites her army against the Spanish invasion, vowing to lead them as their general and share their fate, inspiring resolute cheers before battle.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Queen Elizabeth addressing her army before battle
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Elizabeth, in silver armor on a white horse, delivers a speech vowing to live or die with her soldiers and assumes command as their general.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To advance and lead the army against the Spanish invasion
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Armored on horseback, ready to advance with the army
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Impending battle with Spanish forces
evidence_quotes: riding a white horse, dressed in silver armour, holding a silver staff - Elizabeth
My loving people! We see the sails of the enemy approaching.
So let us sound the advance and go forward, together, you and I.
SOLDIERS Aye!

relation_updates: Becomes field general to the army
status_updates: Assumes military command
Publicly commits to share soldiers' fate
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Silver-armored queen on white horse

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Elizabeth begins her reign by defending Mary Stuart and establishing religious tolerance, while privately confronting her aging and isolation. As threats from Spain and conspiracies mount, she navigates marriage diplomacy, survives an assassination attempt, and faces the moral dilemma of executing Mary Stuart. Ultimately, she realizes her manipulation, takes command against the Spanish Armada, and delivers a rousing speech as a military leader, solidifying her role as the Virgin Queen who protects England.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Raleigh dramatically lays his cloak for Queen Elizabeth, who steps over it skeptically; amused Bess watches as Elizabeth enters the chapel, leaving Raleigh to reflect on his failed gesture.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Courtier attempting to gain royal favor by laying his cloak
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Raleigh drops his cloak in the Queen's path, claiming a puddle, but she steps over it without acknowledgment.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To attract Queen Elizabeth's attention and favor
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Raleigh picks up his cloak and watches Queen Elizabeth enter the chapel, having failed to gain her notice.
evidence_quotes: She stares at Raleigh, then she looks down at the cloak. No sign of any puddles.
He picks up the cloak, and stands gazing after the Queen

relation_updates: Queen Elizabeth coolly dismisses Raleigh's attempt
status_updates: Publicly attempted and failed to gain Queen's favor
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Raleigh, an ambitious courtier whose dramatic gesture is ignored

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Raleigh presents gold taken from a Spanish ship to Elizabeth, who acknowledges it as piracy; he defends it as protecting England, earns her approval, and is welcomed home despite her teasing about his attire.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Petitioner presenting New World discoveries and requesting colonial warrant from Queen Elizabeth
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Raleigh enters with natives and gifts, is accused of piracy by the Spanish Ambassador, kneels before Elizabeth, and requests royal warrant to found a colony in Virginia, gaining her amused attention.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To obtain Elizabeth's permission and royal warrant for establishing an English colony in Virginia
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Has presented his petition and received Elizabeth's welcome for the natives, but the royal warrant for colonization is not yet granted.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Queen Elizabeth will grant the royal warrant for colonization in Virginia
evidence_quotes: Raleigh now enters with Calley, the native Americans and court servants carrying hampers.
Raleigh kneels before the Queen. Elizabeth gestures for him to rise.

relation_updates: Establishes formal relationship with Queen Elizabeth
Incurs hostility from Spanish Ambassador
status_updates: From returning voyager to recognized court petitioner
Gains Elizabeth's attention and provisional favor
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Courtier using flattery and New World displays to seek colonial favor amid political accusations

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Elizabeth confides in Walsingham, calling the Archduke a sweet boy and expressing concern she does not want him harmed by Walsingham's schemes, adding tension beneath courtly decorum.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Courtier seeking royal favor to fund New World ventures
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Raleigh tells Bess his ambition for money, fame, and love, and receives advice to win the Queen's favor through plain truth instead of flattery.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Secure Queen's funding for a New World colony to achieve fame and love
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Has articulated his ambitions to Bess and gained her as a strategic advisor; Elizabeth has taken notice of him
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether the truth-telling strategy will work with Queen Elizabeth
evidence_quotes: RALEIGH What every man wants. Money. Fame. Love.

relation_updates: Bess becomes strategic advisor
Elizabeth observes Raleigh with interest
status_updates: Established as ambitious courtier with New World plans
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Courtier with a clear, sequential plan for advancement via colonization

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Elizabeth and Raleigh share a poignant, flirtatious exchange about love and connection, acknowledging deep mutual understanding; they race back to court, with Elizabeth watching him depart, underscoring unspoken affection and emotional restraint.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Raleigh in a candid conversation with Elizabeth after a horse race
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Elizabeth describes Raleigh's desire for a true companion and states they can be meaningful to each other
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To be known as he is by someone
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Elizabeth watches Raleigh ride away after their exchange
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: What their connection will become
evidence_quotes: We can be something to each other, I hope, Mr. Raleigh.
Elizabeth turns and looks after him as he rides away.

relation_updates: Elizabeth demonstrates deep understanding of Raleigh's desire for a true companion
Elizabeth suggests they can be meaningful to each other
status_updates: Raleigh is now a confidant and potential romantic interest to the Queen
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Confidant to the Queen

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Elizabeth confides in Raleigh about her need for admiration and longing for adventure, urging him not to leave as tensions rise.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Courtier seeking royal warrant for New World expedition
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Elizabeth knights Raleigh and appoints him Captain of her personal guard, preventing his departure to the New World.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Knighted as Sir Walter Raleigh and bound as Captain of the Queen's Guard to court service.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Raleigh's desire to sail to the New World remains unfulfilled.
evidence_quotes: ELIZABETH Rise, Sir Walter Raleigh.
RALEIGH You know why.

relation_updates: Elizabeth reveals emotional vulnerability and dependence on Raleigh
status_updates: Knighted as Sir Walter Raleigh
Appointed Captain of the Queen's Guard
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Knight and Captain of the Queen's Guard

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Raleigh and Bess secretly marry with a priest and maid of honor Margaret present; Raleigh places the ring on each finger while reciting vows, ending on the ring finger.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Groom in a secret marriage ceremony
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Raleigh marries Bess in a secret ceremony, reciting vows and placing a ring on her finger.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Married to Bess after completing the wedding vows.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The marriage is conducted in secret, with only one witness present.
evidence_quotes: Raleigh and Bess are being married in secret by a PRIEST.
Raleigh makes his vow tenderly and lovingly, his eyes on Bess throughout.
RALEIGH ... and with all my worldly goods H I thee endow.
And finally onto her ring finger.

relation_updates: Marriage to Bess Throckmorton (secret)
status_updates: Married man (secretly)
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Secret husband

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Elizabeth, confronted by Raleigh's quiet courage, regains composure and orders Raleigh's arrest for seducing and marrying Bess without consent, asserting royal authority.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Raleigh intervenes after Elizabeth assaults Bess, defending his wife and marriage.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Raleigh's quiet confrontation calms Elizabeth's rage, leading her to order his arrest for secret marriage.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Raleigh is arrested on Elizabeth's orders.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Raleigh faces legal charges for marrying without royal consent.
evidence_quotes: RALEIGH This is not the Queen I love and serve.
His gaze is so unflinching that slowly the madness drains out of her.

relation_updates: Raleigh's relationship with Elizabeth becomes adversarial
Raleigh's bond with Bess is affirmed as he defends her honor
status_updates: Raleigh is arrested on Elizabeth's orders
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Calm defender challenging royal authority.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Raleigh receives a letter, then instructs someone to inform the Queen he will join his ship.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Raleigh receives a letter and declares he will join his ship.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Raleigh reads a letter and decides to rejoin his naval duties.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Raleigh has instructed that the Queen be informed of his decision to join his ship.
evidence_quotes: A Royal Official has just handed him a letter.
He reads it
Tell the Queen I will join my ship.

status_updates: Raleigh is no longer imprisoned
Raleigh is now able to join the fleet

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Raleigh sets fire to the Tyger's pitch by lighting fuses on the prow, then escapes by climbing down the ship's rigging as it sails toward the Spanish fleet.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Raleigh executing the fire ship attack on the Tyger
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Raleigh lights the fuses on the Tyger's prow, ignites the deck pitch, and escapes by scaling down the rigging as the ship sails toward the Spanish fleet.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Attack the Spanish fleet with the burning Tyger
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: The Tyger is ablaze and sailing toward the Spanish fleet, with Raleigh having escaped
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Effectiveness of the fire attack on Spanish ships is unknown
evidence_quotes: He lights the fuses on the prow
He runs to the side of the ship and scales down the rigging.

status_updates: Executed fire ship attack
Successfully escaped burning ship
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Naval commander personally conducting a fire ship assault

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Queen Elizabeth blesses Raleigh's newborn son, reflecting on her role as a mother to her people and valuing their love above all else.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: New father
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Queen Elizabeth blesses Raleigh's newborn son and silently forgives Bess during a royal visit.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To continue dreaming of the New World
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Raleigh's newborn son has been blessed by the Queen, and Bess has been forgiven.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The Queen's lingering displeasure with Raleigh's marriage and child
evidence_quotes: Now in his solitary dance Raleigh turns, and we see he's holding a new-born BABY BOY.
The Queen puts out her hand and touches Bess lightly on one cheek. A silent forgiveness.
Elizabeth takes the baby in her arms. She holds him carefully.
RALEIGH More than ever.

relation_updates: Queen blesses his son
Queen silently forgives Bess
status_updates: Becomes a father
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Father whose family receives royal forgiveness

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Raleigh begins as an ambitious courtier seeking royal favor through dramatic gestures and colonial ventures. He gains Elizabeth's attention and trust through storytelling and political cunning, becoming her confidant while secretly marrying Bess Throckmorton. After his secret marriage is discovered, he is imprisoned but later released to help defend against the Spanish Armada, ultimately achieving reconciliation when Elizabeth blesses his newborn son.
